behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all target files, the new command names (list, close-all, kill-all), -b flag, 'Browser' terminology, [open]/[closed] markers, and the 'browsers' category rename.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what should change (command names, terminology) without giving line-level code replacements.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The -b flag test explicitly accepts yargs alias OR manual normalization, and other tests check behavioral/textual requirements that any correct fix would satisfy.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Node scripts that parse and validate source structure (regex on declareCommand, category types, switch cases); while they inspect source rather than runtime behavior, they use execution-based checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require both presence of new names AND absence of old ones (e.g., forbidden.filter check), so a stub can't pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors mentioned in instruction (command renames, -b flag, category, Browser messages, [open]/[closed]) have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass behavioral, documentation) and named clearly by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is CLI/code refactor, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and command names are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Files are named at module level (e.g., program.ts) which is reasonable scope guidance, no line numbers cited.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code+doc edit task, not an agent-config edit task.
